title: Learning Motion Feasibility from Point Clouds in Cluttered Environments
abstract: arXiv:2606.26700v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Motion feasibility prediction plays a central role in robotics, particularly in task and motion planning and manipulation. A major bottleneck for this problem in cluttered environments is that infeasible planning attempts by Sampling-based motion planners (SBMPs) can incur substantial computational cost. Also existing approaches for infeasibility certification are limited to low-dimensional configuration spaces and often assume simplified geometric environments represented by primitive objects with known parameters. We study the complementary problem of learning motion feasibility prediction directly from raw RGB-D observations for a 7-DOF manipulator operating in realistic cluttered scenes. We introduce the first large-scale benchmark for this setting, comprising 2.7M grasp feasibility labels over 88 scanned objects and 190 cluttered tabletop scenes. We benchmark three representative classifier families spanning MLP- based, volumetric

translated_title: 从杂乱环境中的点云学习运动可行性
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26700v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：运动可行性预测在机器人学中扮演着核心角色，特别是在任务与运动规划以及操作中。在杂乱环境中，该问题的一个主要瓶颈是，基于采样的运动规划器（SBMPs）进行的不可行规划尝试可能会产生巨大的计算成本。此外，现有的不可行性认证方法仅限于低维配置空间，并且通常假设由具有已知参数的原始对象表示的简化几何环境。我们研究了直接从未经处理的RGB-D观测中学习运动可行性预测的互补性问题，针对在现实杂乱场景中运行的7自由度机械臂。我们引入了该设置下的首个大规模基准，包含270万个抓取可行性标签，涵盖88个扫描对象和190个杂乱桌面场景。我们对三个代表性分类器系列进行了基准测试，包括基于MLP、体积型以及点云型分类器。
tldr: 本文针对7自由度机械臂在杂乱环境中的运动可行性预测问题，首次构建了包含270万标签的大规模基准，并直接利用原始RGB-D观测数据而非简化几何模型进行学习。
